content:
# elizaos-plugins/registry Weekly Report (Apr 19 - 25, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
The week of April 19–25, 2026, was defined by the continued expansion of the ElizaOS ecosystem through the onboarding of new community-developed plugins. Development efforts focused on registry growth, specifically integrating new packages and evaluating proposals for specialized Web3 functionality. The project maintains a steady trajectory of modular growth, with contributors actively extending the framework's capabilities into new domains like Solana-based escrow and reputation systems.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### Registry Expansion and Integration
The primary focus this week was the formal integration of new plugins into the registry index. These additions ensure that the ElizaOS framework remains modular and accessible to a broader range of agent architectures.
*   **Plugin Onboarding:** The registry successfully integrated `@elisym/plugin-elizaos` ([#346](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/346)), which involved verifying NPM package naming conventions and ensuring accurate GitHub repository referencing.
*   **New Proposals:** The ecosystem is expanding into specialized Web3 use cases, evidenced by the submission of `@holdfastprotocol/eliza-plugin` ([#348](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/348)). This proposed plugin aims to introduce Solana-focused escrow and reputation functionality, marking a significant step toward deeper Web3 integration for ElizaOS agents.
*   **Pending Submissions:** The registry is currently reviewing the addition of `@error403agent/elizaos-plugin-deepblue` ([#347](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/347)), which remains in the queue for integration.

## 🐛 Issues & Triage
*   **Closed Issues:** There were no issues closed during this reporting period.
*   **New & Active Issues:** No new issues were opened, and there are currently no active issues tracked in the repository. The project's current workflow is primarily driven by pull request submissions for registry updates rather than bug tracking or feature requests via the issue tracker.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
The community remains active in contributing to the registry's growth, with multiple external developers submitting plugins for inclusion. The collaboration dynamic is currently centered on the PR review process, ensuring that new plugins meet the registry's standards for naming and repository structure. The influx of diverse plugin submissions—ranging from general agent plugins to specific Web3 financial modules—indicates a healthy and growing interest in the ElizaOS framework.
